element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial level designed to teach core mechanics with minimal threat
Collectible introduced in safe context, teaches observation and reward loop
Hit from below to reveal coins or power-ups; teaches interaction with environment
Simple enemy defeated by jumping on it; introduces core combat pattern
Grants size increase and brick-breaking ability; protective against one hit
Retracts into shell when jumped on, can be used as projectile weapon
Offensive power-up enabling fireball attacks; shifts from evasion to aggression
Temporary invincibility item; grants temporary dominance over enemies
Reward exploration and experimentation; contain coins or rare items
Hidden reward for thorough exploration; extends play session
Instant death hazard; teaches spatial awareness and precision
Final boss; culmination of all learned mechanics and power-ups

typical_relief_triggers: Finding hidden coins in question mark bricks
Gaining Super Mushroom protection after vulnerability
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation with safe tutorial introduction (World 1-1), steady difficulty curve across worlds, punctuated by power-up relief moments and enemy encounters. Time pressure provides consistent tension baseline.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Question mark bricks marked with visible ? symbol indicate interactive elements; some bricks are invisible, signaling hidden secrets to explorers
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Invisible/hidden bricks containing coins and rare items reward experimentation and systematic jumping; encourages thorough level exploration beyond mandatory path

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-focused (World 1-1) with minimal hazards; introduces jumping, coins, basic enemies, and first power-up. Safe learning environment with Goomba as gentle threat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Steady introduction of new enemy types, pit hazards, and hidden content. Player gains access to Fire Flower and invincibility items. Increased spatial complexity and enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dense enemy layouts, frequent pit hazards, and complex platforming sequences. Culminates in Bowser encounter requiring mastery of all prior mechanics and optimal power-up usage
notable_spikes: Introduction of pit hazards (instant death vs. recoverable damage)
Transition from Goomba-only to mixed enemy encounters
World 8 / Bowser boss sequence (requires precise execution and power-up management)

notable_level_patterns.description: World 1-1 serves as explicit tutorial introducing core mechanics (jump, collect coins, hit question mark bricks, encounter first enemy) in safe context with minimal lethal hazards. Establishes learning framework for all subsequent levels

Progression gates access to advanced strategies through power-up availability. Fire Flower enables offensive play; Starman provides temporary dominance windows; Super Mushroom provides damage buffering. Creates distinct tactical phases within levels

Hidden coins and rare items in invisible bricks incentivize systematic exploration and jumping. Players must balance speed (time pressure) against thoroughness (hidden rewards), creating dynamic play styles
Successive enemy defeats without ground contact grant extra lives, rewarding skilled players and creating tension-building momentum sequences
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-late game levels with multiple enemies

sources_used: wikipedia (Super Mario Bros. article - Gameplay section, Development/World 1-1 subsection)
